steps.title: Build Something Real — Something Worth Paying For
steps.body:
Week 2's destination: one complete, valuable deliverable — something you could charge $100-500 for.

This isn't an exercise. This is your portfolio piece.

Choose one:
• A full content package (blog + social + email + landing page)
• A competitive research brief with strategic recommendations
• A complete go-to-market plan
• A working web app or tool
• A brand kit with visual assets
• A complete marketing campaign

Everything you've learned Days 8-13 feeds into this. Combine the skills.

What makes a $100-500 deliverable:

• It solves a real problem for a real person or business
• It's complete — not a draft, not a template, but a finished asset
• It demonstrates multiple skills (research + writing + design + strategy)
• It's deliverable as a PDF, URL, or working link
• The buyer could use it immediately with no further work

If it doesn't meet these criteria, it's not capstone-ready.

steps.title: Real Capstone Examples From Previous Cohorts
steps.body:
Example 1 — Content Package ($200):
A real estate agent got a complete content package: 4 blog posts targeting first-time homebuyers, 20 social media posts, 2 email nurture sequences, and a landing page redesign. The agent used it for 3 months of content marketing.

Example 2 — Research Brief ($350):
A SaaS founder needed competitive intelligence for a board presentation. They got a 12-page research brief covering 8 competitors, market sizing, feature comparison tables, pricing analysis, and strategic recommendations.

Example 3 — Web App ($500):
A freelance consultant got a custom client portal app: intake form, project status dashboard, file upload, and automated invoice generation. Built with Cursor AI, deployed on Vercel.

Example 4 — Brand Kit ($150):
A startup founder got a complete visual identity: logo variations, color palette, typography guide, social media templates, and brand guidelines document.

All built by non-coders using AI tools.

steps.title: Plan Your Capstone Project
steps.instruction:
This is your Week 2 capstone plan. Be specific — this should be executable tomorrow.

Capstone Type: [Content | Research | Strategy | App | Brand | Campaign]

Project Description: [What are you building, and for whom?]

Value: [$100-500 — why is it worth this?]

Skills Used: [Which Days 8-13 skills combine here?]

AI Tools Needed: [Which platforms will you use?]

Delivery Format: [PDF, URL, live app, design files, etc.]

Timeline: [How long will it take?]
steps.placeholder:
Capstone Type: Content Package + Marketing Campaign

Project: Complete launch package for a local coffee shop's new cold brew line. Includes: brand story, product description, 10 social media posts, email announcement to their list, press release, in-store signage copy.

Value: $200 — saves the owner 20+ hours of content creation and produces professional marketing materials

Skills Used: Content system (Day 8), Marketing campaign (Day 11), Design prompts (Day 13)

AI Tools: ChatGPT for copy, Canva AI for visuals, Claude for content strategy

Delivery: One PDF package + Canva link with editable templates

Timeline: 4 hours of focused work

questions.prompt: What's the most marketable skill you've gained in Week 2? If you had to sell one AI-powered service starting tomorrow, what would it be?
questions.placeholder: Your most marketable skill and how you'd sell it...

questions.prompt: Week 3 starts automation — make AI work without you. What's one thing you built this week that you'd most like to put on autopilot?

steps.title: Week 2 Complete!
steps.body:
You've completed the 'Real Work' week. You're no longer someone who uses AI for occasional help — you're someone who produces real, valuable deliverables with AI.

Week 2 Summary:
• Day 8: Content systems — one idea, every format
• Day 9: Research — deep briefs in minutes
• Day 10: Strategy — SWOT, roadmaps, GTM plans
• Day 11: Marketing — campaigns at scale
• Day 12: Coding — apps without coding
• Day 13: Design — professional visuals
• Day 14: Capstone — your $100-500 project

Next week: Automation. Make AI work without you. Pipelines, APIs, triggers, and continuous operation.
